question:
Will Russia gain more territory than it loses in June 2026?

Resolution criteria: Resolves to the outcome of the question found at https://manifold.markets/a_l_e_x/will-russia-gain-more-territory-tha-pPnUl9s9Ig.

Background: Resolution criteria

This market will resolve YES if, according to a majority of the resolution sources, Russia achieves a net territorial gain in June 2026.

This market will resolve NO if, according to a majority of the resolution sources, Russia does not achieve a net territorial gain in June 2026.

Resolution sources

The resolution sources are:

Black Bird Group

AMK Mapping

Divgen

Close date

This market is set to close at 23:59 UTC on 7 July 2026, although it may resolve sooner.

Clarifications

This market will be voided if two or more resolution sources fail to provide an estimate before the close date.

evidences.title: Ukraine in maps: Tracking the war with Russia - BBC
evidences.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin says he wants this buffer zone to protect Russia, after Ukrainian forces captured a swathe of territory further north in Kursk in the summer of 2024. Russian forces eventually drove them out, with the help of North Korean troops. Side-by-side comparison map showing changes in territorial control just over the Russian border between Russia and Ukraine from 16 August 2024 to 8 September 2025. Left map (2024): Limit of Ukrainian advances are marked with purple stripes Right map (2025): A slightly larger area including small areas across the Ukrainian border is shaded with a bit more in yellow to show claimed Russian control. Key towns labeled include Kursk, Tetkino, and Sumy. Source: ISW [...] Russian ground troops moved in quickly and within a few weeks were in control of large areas of Ukraine and had advanced to the suburbs of Kyiv. Russian forces were bombarding Kharkiv, and had taken territory in the east and south as far as Kherson, and surrounded the port city of Mariupol. Series of four maps showing changes in Russian military control in Ukraine from February 2022 to August 2025. [...] A Russian summer offensive near the eastern town of Pokr...

evidences.title: Mapping Russian attacks and territorial gains across Ukraine
evidences.content: Yes, keep me updated Moscow’s forces concentrated fighting around the logistical hub of Kostiantynivka, which they entered in December, in a bid to gain control of Kramatorsk and Sloviansk – the last major cities under Ukrainian control in Donetsk. In December, the ISW estimated Russian advances had claimed no more than 5 percent of Kostiantynivka. In the north, Ukraine managed to hold off Russia’s yearlong campaign for Kupiansk in the Kharkiv region with a surprise counteroffensive in December. The map below shows four years of territorial changes on the ground, highlighting Russia’s gains and Ukraine’s efforts to regain territory. Image 3: INTERACTIVE - UKRAINE - GAINS - FEB 23, 2026 copy-1771917424 (Al Jazeera) ## Air and drone attacks have increased threefold

evidences.content: Image 15: Map Thumbnail Putin glazed over the Russian Eastern Grouping of Forces’ offensive operations in southern Ukraine and dismissed Ukraine’s 2026 advances in southern Ukraine. Putin claimed that the Russian Eastern Grouping of Forces is advancing between one and 1.3 kilometers per day in the Zaporizhia sector. Putin outright denied any recent Ukrainian territorial liberations. Putin tried to cover up Ukrainian battlefield successes, dismissing them as Ukrainian sabotage and reconnaissance groups that occasionally infiltrate into the Russian near rear dressed as Russian servicemembers but quickly face elimination. ISW has observed evidence to assess that Ukrainian forces have liberated over 400 square kilometers of territory in the Oleksandrivka direction since January 1, 2026. evidences.content: Ukrainian forces continue targeting Russian artillery and drone operators in east (left) bank Kherson Oblast. The commander of a Ukrainian artillery battery reported on June 20 that Russian forces are continuing to shell the west (right) bank Kherson Oblast but had to move their artillery systems deeper in the rear due to the constant threat from Ukrainian artillery and first-person view (FPV) strikes. The commander noted that Russian forces are carefully camouflaging their towed guns near Oleshkivsky Pisky (around 30 kilometers east of Kherson City) and that Ukrainian forces are targeting Russian drone operators. The commander implied that Russian towed guns and drone operators hiding in dense urban areas are harder for Ukrainian forces to detect and disable. The commander added that [...] Map Thumbnail Map Thumbnail Ukrainian and Russian forces continue targeting each other’s frontline drone positions in the Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka tactical area. evidences.title: Ukraine war situation update | 6 – 12 June 2026
evidences.content: During the week of 6 to 12 June, Russian forces attacked at least 14 petrol stations in Ukraine’s front line regions, in a trend that had been intensifying since April. On 9 June alone, Russian drones damaged three different petrol stations in the villages of Korobochkyne, Malynivka, and Pechenihy in the Kharkiv region. In the Chernihiv region, Russian drones attacked petrol stations in Koriukivka and Novhorod-Siversky. They also attacked the cities of Nikopol and Pavlohrad and the villages of Bohdanivka and Hrushivka in the Dnipropetrovsk region, as well as a petrol station in the city of Kherson. In some of these strikes, Russian forces launched small drones at targets 20 to 25 kilometers within the front line. They mostly caused fires to erupt, without inflicting mass casualties. This [...] ReliefWeb logo ## Content Search Ukraine # Ukraine war situation update | 6 – 12 June 2026 Ukraine Conflict Monitor Update Russia steps up its targeting of critical civilian infrastructure along Ukraine’s front line regions. 1,937 political violence events 3% increase compared to last week 205 incidents of violence targeting civilians 6% decrease compared to last week At least 61 civilians...

evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 24, 2026 | ISW
evidences.content: Ukrainian and Russian forces continue conducting ground attacks in the Novopavlivka and Oleksandrivka direction but have not been able to make significant tactical gains thus far. Mashovets reported that Ukrainian forces counterattacked north and south of the N-15 Zaporizhia-Donetsk highway in late May and early June and that Russian forces are currently trying to prevent further Ukrainian advances in the area. Mashovets noted that Russian forces unsuccessfully attempted to advance along the Orikhove-Novopavlivka line and push Ukrainian forces to the north from eastern Novopavlivka and to infiltrate to the north from Filiya (south of Novopavlivka). Mashovets reported that Russian forces remain in southern Novopavlivka, north of the Solena River, and east of Ivanivka – the same positions [...] Ukrainian interdiction of Russian logistics in the Kupyansk direction is inflicting heavy Russian losses that are forcing Russian forces to change their offensive tactics. evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 13, 2026 | ISW
evidences.content: Russian forces conducted a series of long-range drone strikes against Ukraine on the night of June 12 to 13. The Ukrainian Air Force reported that Russian forces launched 118 Shahed-type, Gerbera-type, and Italmas-type strike drones, Banderol-type loitering munitions, and Parodiya-type decoy drones from the directions of Kursk and Oryol cities; Millerovo, Rostov Oblast; Primorsko-Akhtarsk, Krasnodar Krai; occupied Donetsk City, and occupied Hvardiiske, Crimea. The Ukrainian Air Force reported that Ukrainian forces downed 110 drones, that three drones struck three locations, and that debris fell on six locations. Ukrainian officials reported that Russian forces struck civilian, residential, and commercial infrastructure in Dnipropetrovsk, Mykolaiv, and Sumy oblasts. [...] Ukrainian forces continue their intermediate-range strike campaign against Russian military assets in occupied Donetsk Oblast on the night of June 12 to 13 and previously on June 5. The Ukrainian General Staff reported that Ukrainian forces struck a Russian command post and a manpower concentration near occupied Soledar (roughly22 kilometers from the frontline) and a manpower concentration near occupied Uspenivk...

evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 23, 2026
evidences.content: Russian gains in Kostyantynivka remain limited to small group infiltrations that are not resulting in consolidated territorial control. Russian President Vladimir Putin claimed on June 23 that Russian forces are advancing on all areas of the frontline and that Russian forces are “practically reaching” Kostyantynivka. Putin dismissed Ukrainian official statements that the city is a contested “gray zone” but acknowledged that Ukrainian forces maintain positions in buildings and are counterattacking. The Russian MoD continued on June 23 its recent pattern of publishing hyper-tactical details of alleged Russian gains in Kostyantynivka in order to aggrandize Russia’s progress. Ukraine’s tactical situation in Kostyantynivka is deteriorating, but the Russian MoD and Putin are falsely framing the [...] 4. Signal repeaters on the Belarusian-Ukrainian border enable Russian forces to conduct precision drone strikes against the Ukrainian deep-rear and therefore would represent a legitimate military target for potential Ukrainian strikes. 5. Belarus has materially supported Russia’s war in Ukraine since 2022 and is effectively a cobelligerent with Russia. 6. The Kremlin may attempt to invoke...

evidences.content: Ukraine's intermediate-range and frontline strike campaigns against Russian ground lines of communication (GLOCs) are reportedly disrupting Russian supplies of ammunition and drones to the Hulyaipole direction. The commander of a Ukrainian regiment operating in the Hulyaipole direction reported on June 23 that Ukrainian strikes against Russian GLOCs in southern Ukraine, including against the Chonhar Bridge connecting occupied Kherson Oblast and Crimea, have significantly reduced the amount of supplies Russian forces operating in Zaporizhia Oblast are receiving from Crimea.( The commander reported that Russian forces must travel 50 kilometers on foot to bring supplies to the front and noted that even motorcycles are not efficient as they often hit mines. evidences.content: Russian forces continued limited ground operations in western Zaporizhia Oblast on June 18 and 19 but did not make confirmed advances. A Russian milblogger claimed on June 18 that Russian forces recently advanced beyond Mala Tokmachka (southeast of Orikhiv), but the spokesperson for a Ukrainian brigade operating in the area reported on June 19 that Russian forces do not even hold positions within Mala Tokmachka and advanced at closest 300 meters from the settlement before Ukrainian forces repelled the Russian forces. A prominent Kremlin-affiliated Russian milblogger posted a map on June 19 acknowledging that the Russian Forward Line of Own Troops (FLOT) is further west and south of Nesteryanka (southeast of Zaporizhzhia City) than ISW previously assessed, though these Ukrainian gains are [...] ISW previously assessed, though these Ukrainian gains are not within the last 24 hours. evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 25, 2026 | ISW
evidences.content: Ukrainian forces are interdicting Russian logistics and communications in the Pokrovsk direction to degrade Russian offensive capabilities. The Ukrainian 7th Rapid Reaction Corps reported on June 25 that Russian forces continue to concentrate forces and resources in Pokrovsk in an attempt to use Pokrovsk as a logistics hub but that Ukrainian forces are hampering Russian attempts to advance by striking their communication systems and drone launch points. The 7th Corps noted that periodic Russian communication losses result in drone control disruptions, meaning that Russian forces have fewer drones operating at a given time, reducing Russia’s ability to advance. Map Thumbnail [...] Russian forces are actively using strike drones to target Ukrainian logistics but are struggling to advance in the Lyman direction. The commander of a Ukrainian battalion operating in the Lyman direction reported on June 25 that the situation in the Lyman direction remains stable and that Russian forces are not making tactical gains. The commander noted that Russian forces continue to actively use strike drones of various types capable of operating at greater depths and reach Ukrainian logistics routes...

evidences.title: Ukraine says its long-range drone campaign has reached a new ...
evidences.content: ## Euromaidan Press's Post ### Euromaidan Press 1d · Ukraine says its long-range drone campaign has reached a new scale, with deep strikes inside Russia increasing by 1,150% since the start of 2026, according to the Unmanned Systems Forces. In June alone, Ukrainian drone units carried out 2,359 deep-strike missions targeting sites 500–2,000 km behind the front line, hitting 172 military-industrial and energy facilities. The military says its operations are now focused on Russia’s defense industry, oil refineries, logistics hubs, ammunition depots, and command infrastructure, alongside continued pressure on occupied Crimea. Officials say the aim is to reduce Russia’s ability to sustain its war effort by hitting production and supply systems far from the battlefield.

[...] tactical progress. ISW continues to assess that Ukraine’s ability to defend Kostyantynivka is deteriorating but that Russian sources appear to be curating their claims about Russian advances in order to create the perception that the Russian presence in the city is more widespread than it actually is. [...] Russia’s increasingly large and diverse strike packages highlight the critical importance of Western assistance in bolstering Ukraine’s air defense capabilities. Russian forces consistently deploy Kh-101, Iskander-M, Iskander-K, and Shahed/Geran drones to strike Ukraine and will likely use these technological adaptations to increase the scale and scope of the damage such strikes inflict. US-made Patriot air defense systems that can effectively counter Russian ballistic missiles remain critical for Ukrainian air defenses. Ukraine has also been trying to secure produc...

evidences.title: AMK Mapping (Telegram channel) — Grokipedia
evidences.content: AMK Mapping provides real-time reports on Russian missile and drone strikes targeting Ukrainian infrastructure and cities, often detailing launch types, trajectories, and impacts within specific oblasts. These updates include summaries of attack scales alongside assessments of resulting damage. The channel tracks airstrikes and troop movements, highlighting Russian Su-57 operations in contested areas like Kursk Oblast, while incorporating OSINT-derived geolocations to map incursion points and defensive responses. Maps visualize frontline shifts and strike zones, emphasizing Ukrainian advances or Russian setbacks in event selections that align with a pro-Ukraine viewpoint.(#ref-5)](#ref-5) Coverage extends to Ukrainian offensive actions, such as tactical aircraft deploying Storm Shadow [...] AMK Mapping provides real-time updates on Middle East conflicts, including airstrikes, ballistic missile launches, and troop movements in hotspots such as Israel, Iran, Lebanon, and the West Bank.(#ref-6)](#ref-6)(#ref-7)](#ref-7)(#ref-8)](#ref-8) This coverage integrates OSINT-sourced imagery and geolocation data to track events like Israeli operations in southern Lebanon and Iranian strikes...

evidences.content: Skip to Content BLACK BIRD GROUP BLACK BIRD GROUP # BLACK BIRD GROUP ### About our organization Black Bird Group is an independent, non-governmental security analysis and intelligence organization. evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 8, 2026 | Critical Threats
evidences.content: Click here to see ISW’s 3D control of terrain topographic map of Ukraine. Use of a computer (not a mobile device) is strongly recommended for using this data-heavy tool. Click here to access ISW’s archive of interactive time-lapse maps of the Russian invasion of Ukraine. These maps complement the static control-of-terrain map that ISW produces daily by showing a dynamic frontline. ISW will update this time-lapse map archive monthly. For more information on ISW’s map methodology, please view our statement here. [...] on June 7 shows Ukrainian forces striking a Russian fuel tanker near Pelahiivka (roughly 80 kilometers from the frontline); a locomotive near occupied Dovzhansk (roughly 143 kilometers from the frontline); and substations near Hirne (roughly 130 kilometers from the frontline), Pervomaiske (one of multiple settlements between 25 and 124 kilometers from the frontline), and Novookhtryka (roughly 62 kilometers from the frontline).(#_edncf58bdbe7ac0aaf5b74d57d9a663891040) [...] (#_edncf58bdbe7ac0aaf5b74d57d9a6638910ref49) (#_edncf58bdbe7ac0aaf5b74d57d9a6638910ref50) (#_edncf58bdbe7ac0aaf5b74d57d9a6638910ref51) (#_edncf58bdbe7ac0aaf5b74d57d9a6638910ref52) ; ; (#_edncf58bdb...

evidences.title: Arms Trends in Ukraine: 1 June - 7 June, 2026
evidences.content: Ukrainian analysts at Defense Express note that one of the key factors determining whether the system can intercept ballistic targets such as the Iskander-M will be the FP-7.x's ability to perform intensive maneuvers. For effective and reliable interception of ballistic missiles, it is not enough for an air-defence missile to simply explode nearby. It must accurately strike the warhead directly, using the so-called “hit-to-kill” principle. Such a high level of accuracy must be achieved even when the enemy ballistic missile is actively maneuvering at speeds of several Mach. [...] Meanwhile, actual deliveries of Patriot interceptors to Ukraine are already complicated by the fact that the United States wants to replenish its own stockpiles after the war with Iran, during which it reportedly used 45% to 61% of its Patriot interceptor stocks. Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, the UAE, and Saudi Arabia have similar concerns, as they also reportedly fired several thousand Patriot interceptors. According to the analysts, interceptor missiles alone cannot be the answer to Russia’s ballistic missile threat. Without strikes on launchers, missile stockpiles, and production facilities, no missile-defe...

evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 20, 2026 | ISW
evidences.content: Map Thumbnail [...] Map Thumbnail Russian forces continued infiltrations in the Kupyansk direction. Geolocated footage published on June 20 shows two Russian servicemembers infiltrating into central Radkivka (north of Kupyansk). Russian milbloggers claimed on June 20 that Russian forces advanced into Radkivka and northeast and west of Novovasylivka (northeast of Kupyansk). Map Thumbnail [...] Russian forces continue infiltration missions deeper into Kostyantynivka, indicating that Russian forces are likely consolidating at least some positions in the city while interspersed with Ukrainian positions. ISW has updated its control of terrain assessment in Kostyantynivka by expanding Russian infiltration areas and Russian advances in the city. ISW increasingly has observed evidence of Russian forces infiltrating further into northern, northwestern, and western Kostyantynivka in recent days and has not observed evidence of Ukrainian forces maintaining an organized defense in the easternmost and southernmost sections of Kostyantynivka, indicating that Russian forces have likely advanced in southern Kostyantynivka and up to a segment of the northern outskirts of Kostyantynivka.

evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 16, 2026
evidences.content: Russian forces conducted infiltration missions in the Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka tactical area. Geolocated footage published on June 15 shows Ukrainian forces striking a Russian position in southeastern Tykhonivka (northeast of Kostyantynivka) after what ISW assesses was a Russian infiltration mission. Russian milbloggers claimed that Russian forces advanced in central and western Kostyantynivka. Map Thumbnail [...] ## Russian Supporting Effort: Southern Axis #### Russian objective: Maintain frontline positions, secure rear areas against Ukrainian strikes, and advance within tube artillery range of Zaporizhzhia City Russian forces continued offensive operations northwest to southwest of Hulyaipole and in western Zaporizhia Oblast on June 15 and 16 but did not advance. Map Thumbnail [...] ### Russian Subordinate Main Effort #3 – Donetsk Oblast #### Russian objective: Capture the entirety of Donetsk Oblast, the claimed territory of Russia’s proxies in Donbas, and advance into Dnipropetrovsk Oblast Map Thumbnail Map Thumbnail Russian forces continued offensive operations in the Slovyansk direction on June 15 and 16 but did not advance as Ukrainian forces reportedly counterattacked....

evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 17, 2026
evidences.content: Russian forces continued limited offensive operations in the Dobropillya tactical area on June 16 and 17 but did not make confirmed advances. The Russian MoD claimed that elements of the Russian Central Grouping of Forces seized Stepy (east of Dobropillya). A Russian milblogger claimed that Russian forces advanced southwest of Novyi Donbas (east of Dobropillya). Map Thumbnail Russian forces continued offensive operations in the Pokrovsk direction on June 16 and 17 but did not advance. Map Thumbnail Russian forces continued limited offensive operations in the Novopavlivka direction on June 16 and 17 but did not advance. [...] ## Russian Supporting Effort: Northern Axis #### Russian objective: Create defensible buffer zones in Sumy Oblast along the international border Russian forces recently conducted an infiltration mission in northern Sumy Oblast. Geolocated footage published on June 17 shows Ukrainian forces striking Russian positions in central and southern Ryasne (southeast of Sumy City) after what ISW assesses were Russian infiltration missions. The presence of Russian forces in Ryasne indicates that Russian forces likely infiltrated the fields east of the settlement. Map T...
